# Rosalind Problem: Sequence a Peptide (Sequence_a_Peptide)

## Problem Description
Given a DNA string, we need to find the peptide sequence that corresponds to it by translating the DNA into RNA and then into amino acids using the genetic code.

## Solution

```ruby
def translate_dna_to_peptide(dna_string)
  # Genetic code mapping
  codon_table = {
    "TTT" => "F", "TTC" => "F", "TTA" => "L", "TTG" => "L",
    "TCT" => "S", "TCC" => "S", "TCA" => "S", "TCG" => "S",
    "TAT" => "Y", "TAC" => "Y", "TAA" => "*", "TAG" => "*",
    "TGT" => "C", "TGC" => "C", "TGA" => "*", "TGG" => "W",
    "CTT" => "L", "CTC" => "L", "CTA" => "L", "CTG" => "L",
    "CCT" => "P", "CCC" => "P", "CCA" => "P", "CCG" => "P",
    "CAT" => "H", "CAC" => "H", "CAA" => "Q", "CAG" => "Q",
    "CGT" => "R", "CGC" => "R", "CGA" => "R", "CGG" => "R",
    "ATT" => "I", "ATC" => "I", "ATA" => "I", "ATG" => "M",
    "ACT" => "T", "ACC" => "T", "ACA" => "T", "ACG" => "T",
    "AAT" => "N", "AAC" => "N", "AAA" => "K", "AAG" => "K",
    "AGT" => "S", "AGC" => "S", "AGA" => "R", "AGG" => "R",
    "GTT" => "V", "GTC" => "V", "GTA" => "V", "GTG" => "V",
    "GCT" => "A", "GCC" => "A", "GCA" => "A", "GCG" => "A",
    "GAT" => "D", "GAC" => "D", "GAA" => "E", "GAG" => "E",
    "GGT" => "G", "GGC" => "G", "GGA" => "G", "GGG" => "G"
  }
  
  # Convert DNA to RNA (replace T with U)
  rna_string = dna_string.gsub("T", "U")
  
  # Translate RNA to peptide
  peptide = ""
  
  # Process RNA string in groups of 3 nucleotides (codons)
  (0...rna_string.length).step(3) do |i|
    # Check if we have a complete codon
    if i + 2 < rna_string.length
      codon = rna_string[i, 3]
      
      # Stop translation if we encounter a stop codon
      if codon_table[codon] == "*"
        break
      end
      
      peptide += codon_table[codon]
    end
  end
  
  peptide
end

# Alternative more concise solution
def translate_dna_to_peptide_v2(dna_string)
  codon_table = {
    "TTT" => "F", "TTC" => "F", "TTA" => "L", "TTG" => "L",
    "TCT" => "S", "TCC" => "S", "TCA" => "S", "TCG" => "S",
    "TAT" => "Y", "TAC" => "Y", "TAA" => "*", "TAG" => "*",
    "TGT" => "C", "TGC" => "C", "TGA" => "*", "TGG" => "W",
    "CTT" => "L", "CTC" => "L", "CTA" => "L", "CTG" => "L",
    "CCT" => "P", "CCC" => "P", "CCA" => "P", "CCG" => "P",
    "CAT" => "H", "CAC" => "H", "CAA" => "Q", "CAG" => "Q",
    "CGT" => "R", "CGC" => "R", "CGA" => "R", "CGG" => "R",
    "ATT" => "I", "ATC" => "I", "ATA" => "I", "ATG" => "M",
    "ACT" => "T", "ACC" => "T", "ACA" => "T", "ACG" => "T",
    "AAT" => "N", "AAC" => "N", "AAA" => "K", "AAG" => "K",
    "AGT" => "S", "AGC" => "S", "AGA" => "R", "AGG" => "R",
    "GTT" => "V", "GTC" => "V", "GTA" => "V", "GTG" => "V",
    "GCT" => "A", "GCC" => "A", "GCA" => "A", "GCG" => "A",
    "GAT" => "D", "GAC" => "D", "GAA" => "E", "GAG" => "E",
    "GGT" => "G", "GGC" => "G", "GGA" => "G", "GGG" => "G"
  }
  
  # Convert DNA to RNA and translate
  rna = dna_string.tr("T", "U")
  
  # Process in codons and translate until stop codon
  result = ""
  rna.scan(/.{3}/) do |codon|
    amino_acid = codon_table[codon]
    break if amino_acid == "*"
    result += amino_acid
  end
  
  result
end

# Example usage:
# dna = "
